title: Physical Geography
description: Major rivers, mountain ranges, oceans, deserts, and landforms

cards.back: Nile River (~4,130 miles, in Africa)
Northeastern Africa; flows through Uganda, Sudan, and Egypt to the Mediterranean

cards.front: Second longest river in the world
cards.back: Amazon River (~3,976 miles, in South America)
Carries more water than any other river; flows through the world's largest rainforest
Missouri River (~2,341 miles); Mississippi is 2nd but drains a larger basin
Gulf of Mexico (at New Orleans, Louisiana)
China; longest river in Asia (~3,917 miles)
Yangtze River (China)
Second longest river in Europe; flows through 10 countries
Sacred river in Hinduism; flows through India and Bangladesh
Deepest river in the world; in central Africa
Mount Everest (29,032 ft / 8,849 m) in the Himalayas
Border of Nepal and Tibet (China), in the Himalayas
Denali (20,310 ft) in Alaska
Mount Elbrus (18,510 ft) in Russia's Caucasus Mountains
Mount Kilimanjaro (19,341 ft) in Tanzania
Aconcagua (22,837 ft) in Argentina's Andes
Southern Asia; spans Nepal, India, Bhutan, Tibet/China, Pakistan
Western South America; longest continental mountain range (~4,300 miles)
Western North America; from British Columbia to New Mexico
Central Europe; spans France, Switzerland, Austria, Italy, and others
Andes Mountains (~4,300 miles)
Pacific Ocean (covers ~63 million sq miles)
Atlantic Ocean
Arctic Ocean
Pacific, Atlantic, Indian, Southern (Antarctic), Arctic
Deepest point in the ocean (~36,000 ft); in the western Pacific
Challenger Deep in the Mariana Trench (~36,000 ft)
Antarctic Desert (cold desert); largest hot desert is the Sahara
Northern Africa; largest hot desert (~3.6 million sq miles)
Sahara Desert (Northern Africa)
Northern China and southern Mongolia
Off the coast of Queensland, Australia; world's largest coral reef system
Great Barrier Reef (Australia)
Caspian Sea (borders Russia, Kazakhstan, Turkmenistan, Iran, Azerbaijan)

cards.front: Largest freshwater lake by surface area
cards.back: Lake Superior (U.S./Canada)
Lake Baikal (Russia) — over 5,300 ft deep
Superior, Michigan, Huron, Erie, Ontario (SMHEO or 'HOMES')

cards.front: Ring of Fire — what is it?
cards.back: Zone of frequent earthquakes and volcanic eruptions around the Pacific Ocean
Supercontinent that existed ~335–175 million years ago before breaking apart
Large pieces of Earth's crust that slowly move, causing earthquakes and mountain formation
Africa, Antarctica, Asia, Australia/Oceania, Europe, North America, South America
Asia
Australia/Oceania
Russia (~6.6 million sq miles)
South America; mostly in Brazil; largest tropical rainforest
Narrow waterway connecting the Atlantic Ocean to the Mediterranean Sea; separates Europe from Africa
Artificial waterway in Panama connecting the Atlantic and Pacific Oceans
Artificial waterway in Egypt connecting the Mediterranean and Red Seas
